content.full_text:
November 7, 1989


John L. Taylor
Clerk of the Board
City and County of San Francisco
City Hall
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: Your Request for Advice
Advice File No. A-89-487

Dear Mr. Taylor:

We have received your letter requesting advice under the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

The City and County of San Francisco is considering the
adoption of a new conflict of interest code. You have sent us a
copy of the proposed code and asked if we had any comments
concerning is contents. More specifically, you have asked if it
would be possible to require employees to sign a statement, under
penalty of perjury, that the employee has no conflict of interest
and if he or she ever should, that the employee would immediately
notify the city. This statement would be in lieu of the
disclosure required by the conflict of interest code.

CONCLUSION

Local government agencies must adopt and promulgate a
conflict of interest code which will specifically enumerate all
positions within the agency which make or participate in the


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Page Two
November 6, 1989

making of governmental decisions which may foreseeably have a
material effect on an financial interest and for each enumerated
position the code must specify the types of investments, income,
interests in real property and business positions which are
reportable (Sections 87300 and 87302).

It would not be appropriate for employees to merely sign a
document under penalty of perjury stating that no conflicts of
interest exist. Such a statement would not comply with Section
87300 and 87302.

The proposed conflict of interest code for the City and
County of San Francisco meets the provisions of Section 87302.
The disclosure assigned to the designated positions appears to be
tailored to just those financial interests which are administered,
and can be affected, by the designated employees within their
respective division, department or agency. We recommend that the
language requiring the disclosure of business positions be
consistent in every disclosure category.

ANALYSIS

Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code pursuant to the provisions
of this article. A conflict of interest code shall have the force
of law and any violation of a conflict of interest code by a
designated employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter.

Each conflict of interest code shall specifically enumerate
the positions within the agency, which involve the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest and for each such
enumerated position, the specific types of investments, business
positions, interests in real property, and sources of income which
are reportable. Codes shall describe when statements are to be
filed and shall further set forth the circumstances under which
designated employees must disqualify themselves from making,
participating in the making, or using their official position to
influence the making of any decision (Section 87302).

Your code designates numerous boards, commissions,
departments and divisions with your jurisdiction. You have
expended a good deal of time drafting your disclosure categories
to require the disclosure of just those types of financial
interests which your designated employees can affect in their
decision-making. Since we have no reason to know what positions
within your jurisdiction make or participate in making decisions,
we cannot verify that your code covers all decision-makers.

Page Three
November 6, 1989


     The code incorporates Commission Regulation 18730, as the
body of the code, and has attached to it the appendix of
designated positions and accompanying disclosure categories.
Relying on what was possible to review, it appears your conflict
of interest code meets all of the required provisions of section
87302.

     The only substantive comment we have is in regard to the
disclosure of business positions.  The majority of the disclosure
categories appropriately require the disclosure of "business
positions in business entities....".  Category 2 of Sec. 58.125
of the Asian Art Museum requires the disclosure of "...any
position of management....".  You may want to review each of the
disclosure categories to ensure that they are consistent and
require the disclosure of "business positions."

     In your letter you indicated that a member of the board of
supervisors asked if it would be appropriate to merely ask
employees within the city to sign a statement, under penalty of
perjury, that they have no conflict of interest, and that if they
ever did, that they would so notify the city.  It was proposed
that this statement would be in lieu of the filing of the
statement of economic interest required by the conflict of
interest code.

     There is no provision in the Political Reform Act which
allows for such a statement.  As noted earlier, Section 87302
requires agencies to specifically enumerate positions in a
decision-making capacity and requires disclosure of certain
financial interests at specified times.  The in-lieu of statement
suggested by the supervisor would not be in compliance with the
Political Reform Act.

     I hope this has been of assistance to you.  It was a pleasure
to meet you and David Benjamin at our seminar in San Francisco.
If you have any questions, or I can help you further, please feel
free to give me a call at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Jeanette E. Turvill
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Legal Division

KED/jt

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

City Hall
San Francisco 94102
554-5184

August 14, 1989

Jeanette Turvill
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Turvill:

The City and County of San Francisco is considering the adoption of
a new conflict of interest code to accomplish several objectives:

(a)

to adopt the state's standardized code to obtain the obvious
advantages of that procedure.

(b)

to bring up to date our disclosure categories and designated
positions to reflect changes in our organization.

(c)

to put our conflict of interest code into our Administrative
Code, thus making it easier to find and easier to revise than
our present accumulation of resolutions.

(d)

to clarify the designation of the filing officer for positions
when the designation may not be clear.

(e)

to provide administrative provisions to promote awareness of
filing requirements, particularly for initial and leaving
office statements.

In this connection, I have two questions:

First, during a recent discussion of the draft new conflict of
interest code, a member of our Board of Supervisors strongly urged
that we not require City employees (other than perhaps department
heads and other top officers) to disclose economic interests unless
there is probable cause that the employee has a conflict. The
Supervisor suggested that, instead, we require every employee to
sign a statement, under penalty of perjury, that the employee has no
conflict of interest and that if a conflict arises in the future,
either because of changed assignment by the City or changed economic
interests of the employee, the employee will immediately inform the
City.

I enquire of you whether such a system would be in compliance with
the Political Reform Act.

Second, I enquire whether you have any comments for us concerning
the attached draft ordinance.

Your assistance will be appreciated.

This letter is written with the blessing of the office of the City
Attorney with which I have been working closely to improve our code.

Sincerely,

John L. Taylor
Clerk of the Board

cc: Deputy City Attorney
David Benjamin

FILE NO._____________ ORDINANCE NO._____________

[Conflict of Interest Code]

ADOPTING BY REFERENCE AS THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR CERTAIN

AGENCIES OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PROVISIONS OF

THE MODEL CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE ADOPTED BY THE CALIFORNIA FAIR

POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION, PROVIDING FOR THE FILING OF

STATEMENTS OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS, DISCLOSURE CATEGORIES, DESIGNATED

POSITIONS, AND FILING OFFICERS, BY ADDING CHAPTER 58 TO THE SAN

FRANCISCO ADMINISTRATIVE CODE.

Note: All material is new.

Be it ordained by the People of the City and County of San Francisco:

Section 1. The San Francisco Administrative Code is hereby

amended by adding Chapter 58 to read as follows:

Chapter 58. Conflict of Interest Code

Sec. 58.1. Adoption of State Code.

The provisions of Regulation 18730 of the Fair Political

Practices Commission (2 Cal.Admin.Code §18730), as the regulation

reads on the date this ordinance is adopted and as the regulation may

be amended from time to time by the Commission, are hereby adopted and

incorporated herein by this reference as the Conflict of Interest Code

for agencies of the City and County of San Francisco listed in this

chapter commencing with section 58.100.

Sec. 58.2. Copies of Regulation 18730.

Three copies of Regulation 18730 shall be maintained in the

office of the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors.

/ / /

4330g PAGE NO. 1

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS 04/10/89

Sec. 58.3 Filing requirements.

Each officer and employee of the City and County of San

Francisco holding a position designated in this chapter, other than

those officials identified in Sec. 58.600, shall file statements

disclosing the information required by the disclosure categories set

forth in this chapter, on such forms as may be specified by the

California Fair Political Practices Commission (Form 730 unless

otherwise provided by the Commission), and at such times required by

Regulation 18730. A copy of the forms to be used shall be supplied by

the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors to each filing officer.


Sec. 58.4. Filing Officers.

(a) With the exception of those officials identified in Sec.

58.600 and those department heads subordinate to the Public Utilities

Commission, department heads holding designated positions shall file

Statements of Economic Interest with the Clerk of the Board of

Supervisors, who shall be the filing officer for such positions.

(b) Persons in the following designated positions shall file

Statements of Economic Interests with the officer identified in this

subsection, who shall be the filing officer for such positions.

Designated Position                          Filing Officer

Member, Authority Board                      Chief Administrative Officer

Member, Board of Examiners                   Director, Public Works

Member, Hazardous Materials Advisory
Committee                                    Director, Public Health

Staff, Law Library
Department Heads subordinate to
Public Utilities Commission                  Law Librarian
                                            General Manager, Public
                                            Utilities Commission

/ / /

/ / /

/ / /

4330g                                        PAGE NO. 2

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS                         04/10/89

(c) All other persons holding designated positions shall file
with their respective department head or the executive director of
their agency, who shall be the filing officer for such positions.

Sec. 58.5.  Filing Officer Reports.

On or before April 10 of each year, every filing officer shall
submit a written report to the Clerk of the Board setting forth the
names of those persons who are required to file an annual statement
with that filing officer under this chapter but have failed to do so,
or a report stating that all such persons have filed.

Sec. 58.6.  Notice of Appointment.

Whenever the Mayor, the Chief Administrative Officer, or a board
or commission appoints a department head, or receives the resignation
or retirement notice of a department head, the official or the
secretary to the board or commission who makes the appointment or
receives the resignation or retirement notice, shall promptly inform
the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors.  The official or secretary
shall also inform the department head of the necessity to file within
30 days an assuming office of leaving office statement of economic
interests.  Upon receiving notice of the appointment, or the
resignation or retirement, of the department head, the Clerk shall
perform the required duties of the filing officer and obtain the
required statement of economic interests.

Sec. 58.7.  Disclosure Category 1.

For each agency, disclosure category 1 shall read:

"Disclosure Category 1:  Persons in this category shall
disclose all sources of income, interests in real
property, investments, and all business positions in

433"                                                    PAGE NO. 3
BOARD OF SUPERVISORS                                   04/10/89

which the designated employee is a director, officer,

partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of

management."


Sec. 58.8  Disclosure Categories.

For each agency of the City and County of San Francisco,

disclosure categories shall include category 1 as specified in Sec.

58.7, and such additional categories as may be included in the

sections of this chapter applicable to each such agency.


Sec. 58.9.  Definitions.

As used in this chapter:

(a) "Political Reform Act" means the Political Reform Act of

1974, as said Act reads on the date this ordinance is adopted and as

said Act may be amended from time to time.

(b) All other words used in this ordinance shall have the

meanings ascribed to them by the Political Reform Act, if the Act

provides a definition.

sections.question:
The City and County of San Francisco is considering the 
adoption of a new conflict of interest code. 
You have sent us a 
copy of the proposed code and asked if we had any comments 
concerning is contents. 
More specifically, you have asked if it 
would be possible to require employees to sign a statement, under 
penalty of perjury, that the employee has no conflict of interest 
and if he or she ever should, that the employee would immediately 
notify the city. 
This statement would be in lieu of the 
disclosure required by the conflict of interest code.
sections.conclusion:
Local government agencies must adopt and promulgate a 
conflict of interest code which will specifically enumerate all 
positions within the agency which make or participate in the, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
 • (916)322-5660 


Page Two 
November 6, 1989 
making of governmental decisions which may foreseeably have a 
material effect on an financial interest and for each enumerated 
position the code must specify the types of investments, income, 
interests in real property and business positions which are 
reportable (Sections 87300 and 87302). 
It would not be appropriate for employees to merely sign a 
document under penalty of perjury stating that no conflicts of 
interest exist. 
Such a statement would not comply with Section 
87300 and 87302. 
The proposed conflict of interest code for the City and 
County of San Francisco meets the provisions of Section 87302. 
The disclosure assigned to the designated positions appears to be 
tailored to just those financial interests which are administered, 
and can be affected, by the designated employees within their 
respective division, department or agency. 
We recommend that the 
language requiring the disclosure of business positions be 
consistent in every disclosure category.
sections.analysis:
Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt and 
promulgate a conflict of interest code pursuant to the provisions 
of this article. 
A conflict of interest code shall have the force 
of law and any violation of a conflict of interest code by a 
designated employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter. 
Each conflict of interest code shall specifically enumerate 
the positions within the agency, which involve the making or 
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably 
have a material effect on any financial interest and for each such 
enumerated position, the specific types of investments, business 
positions, interests in real property, and sources of income which 
are reportable. 
Codes shall describe when statements are to be 
filed and shall further set forth the circumstances under which 
designated employees must disqualify themselves from making, 
participating in the making, or using their official position to 
influence the making of any decision (Section 87302). 
Your code designates numerous boards, commissions, 
departments and divisions with your jurisdiction. 
You have 
expended a good deal of time drafting your disclosure categories 
to require the disclosure of just those types of financial 
interests which your designated employees can affect in their 
decision-making. 
Since we have no reason to know what positions 
within your jurisdiction make or participate in making decisions, 
we cannot verify that your code covers all decision-makers. 


Page Three 
November 6, 1989 
The code incorporates Commission Regulation 18730, as the 
body of the code, and has attached to it the appendix of 
designated positions and accompanying disclosure categories. 
Relying on what was possible to review, it appears your conflict 
of interest code meets all of the required provisions of section 
87302. 
The only SUbstantive comment we have is in regard to the 
disclosure of business positions. 
The majority of the disclosure 
categories appropriately require the disclosure of "business 
positions in business entities ... ". 
Category 2 with Sec. 58.125 
of the Asian Art Museum requires the disclosure of " ... any 
position of management ... " 
You may want to review each of the 
disclosure categories to ensure that they are consistent and 
require the disclosure of "business positions." 
In your letter you indicated that a member of the board of 
supervisors asked if it would be appropriate to merely ask 
employees within the city to sign a statement, under penalty of 
perjury, that they have no conflict of interest, and that if they 
ever did, that they would so notify the city. 
It was proposed 
that this statement would be in lieu of the filing of the 
statement of economic interest required by the conflict of 
interest code. 
There is no provision in the Political Reform Act which 
allows for such a statement. 
As noted earlier, section 87302 
requires agencies to specifically enumerate positions in a 
decision-making capacity and requires disclosure of certain 
financial interests at specified times. 
The in-lieu of statement 
suggested by the supervisor would not be in compliance with the 
Political Reform Act. 
I hope this has been of assistance to you. 
It was a pleasure 
to meet you and David Benjamin at our seminar in San Francisco.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The City and County of San Francisco is considering the 
adoption of a new conflict of interest code. 
You have sent us a 
copy of the proposed code and asked if we had any comments 
concerning is contents. 
More specifically, you have asked if it 
would be possible to require employees to sign a statement, under 
penalty of perjury, that the employee has no conflict of interest 
and if he or she ever should, that the employee would immediately 
notify the city. 
This statement would be in lieu of the 
disclosure required by the conflict of interest code.

CONCLUSION: Local government agencies must adopt and promulgate a 
conflict of interest code which will specifically enumerate all 
positions within the agency which make or participate in the, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
 • (916)322-5660 


Page Two 
November 6, 1989 
making of governmental decisions which may foreseeably have a 
material effect on an financial interest and for each enumerated 
position the code must specify the types of investments, income, 
interests in real property and business positions which are 
reportable (Sections 87300 and 87302). 
It would not be appropriate for employees to merely sign a 
document under penalty of perjury stating that no conflicts of 
interest exist. 
Such a statement would not comply with Section 
87300 and 87302. 
The proposed conflict of interest code for the City and 
County of San Francisco meets the provisions of Section 87302. 
The disclosure assigned to the designated positions appears to be 
tailored to just those financial interests which are administered, 
and can be affected, by the designated employees within their 
respective division, department or agency. 
We recommend that the 
language requiring the disclosure of business positions be 
consistent in every disclosure category.
embedding.first_500_words: November 7, 1989 John L. Taylor Clerk of the Board City and County of San Francisco City Hall San Francisco, CA 94102 Re: Your Request for Advice Advice File No. A-89-487 Dear Mr. Taylor: We have received your letter requesting advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ QUESTION The City and County of San Francisco is considering the adoption of a new conflict of interest code. You have sent us a copy of the proposed code and asked if we had any comments concerning is contents. More specifically, you have asked if it would be possible to require employees to sign a statement, under penalty of perjury, that the employee has no conflict of interest and if he or she ever should, that the employee would immediately notify the city. This statement would be in lieu of the disclosure required by the conflict of interest code. CONCLUSION Local government agencies must adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code which will specifically enumerate all positions within the agency which make or participate in the 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Page Two November 6, 1989 making of governmental decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on an financial interest and for each enumerated position the code must specify the types of investments, income, interests in real property and business positions which are reportable (Sections 87300 and 87302). It would not be appropriate for employees to merely sign a document under penalty of perjury stating that no conflicts of interest exist. Such a statement would not comply with Section 87300 and 87302. The proposed conflict of interest code for the City and County of San Francisco meets the provisions of Section 87302. The disclosure assigned to the designated positions appears to be tailored to just those financial interests which are administered, and can be affected, by the designated employees within their respective division, department or agency. We recommend that the language requiring the disclosure of business positions be consistent in every disclosure category. ANALYSIS Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code pursuant to the provisions of this article. A conflict of interest code shall have the force of law and any violation of a conflict of interest code by a designated employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter. Each conflict of interest code shall specifically enumerate the positions within the agency, which involve the making or participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest and for each such enumerated position, the specific types of investments, business positions, interests in real property,
